title: Fire Alarm System Annual Test & Inspection Record
description: Mobile-optimized schema for the mandatory CAN/ULC-S536:2019 annual fire alarm inspection and test record. Sections mirror the standard's numbered clauses exactly.

mobile_notes.skip_pattern: If a section has an 'applicable_toggle' and it is set to false, the entire section body should be collapsed/skipped on mobile.

sections.title: Annual Fire Alarm System Test and Inspection Record
sections.description: NOTE: Refer to Section 7.1 of CAN/ULC-S536:2019-REV1

subsections.title: Fire Alarm Annual Test and Inspection Report

The fire alarm system is fully functional.
During the Annual Inspection and Test were any Deficiencies identified? See Page 2, if applicable.

Certificate/ID Number of Secondary Person

The inspection and testing records required by this standard and this section, shall follow a tabular format as shown. The 'Notes' provided in this section are optional in the report. Tests or inspections may not be reworded or revised in order or format. Companies may recreate these required reports for their use, which may contain additions such as a corporate logo as an example.

sections.title: Deficiencies
sections.description: Repeatable table of deficiencies identified during the inspection.

notes_after.label: NOTE: System and device installation locations may differ from those described in CAN/ULC-S534 if a performance based design and alternate solution documents were submitted and approved by the authority having jurisdiction for the system under test.

subsections.title: Deficiencies
subsections.info_text: The Inspection and Testing of any corrections/repairs of deficiencies noted on this form has been completed by qualified personnel in the column marked 'Technician Name & Certificate No.'

item_fields.label: Device Type (See 23.1 Field Device Testing – Legend and Notes)

fields.label: Building Owner / Owner's Representative Name:
fields.group_label: I understand that all Deficiencies in the table above have been corrected

fields.label: Building Owner / Owner's Representative Signature:
Date of Signature (YYYY-MM-DD):
NOTE: Only the above table needs to be updated on the correction of deficiencies. The entire report does not have to be reissued.

For the purposes of this standard, Recommendation is a proposal or suggestion as to the best course of action for improvement of system components or system operation / installation, including safety considerations, such as: a) Identifying antiquated or obselete equipment; b) Availability of newer cost effective technology; or c) Alternate methods of detection.
Technician's Recommendations / Testing Notes

sections.title: Technician Attendance Log
sections.description: Required log of every site visit during the inspection period.

sections.title: Documentation
sections.description: Verify fire alarm system documentation is available or accessible on site.

notes_before.label: NOTE: Refer to Section 7 of CAN/ULC-S536:2019-REV1.

fields.label: A — Instructions for resetting the system and silencing alarm signals.
B — Instructions for silencing the trouble signal and action to be taken when the trouble signal sounds.
C — Description of the function of each operating control and indicator on the fire alarm unit.
D — Description of the area of fire zone protected by each alarm detection circuit (this may be in the form of a list or plan drawing).
E — Description of alarm signal operation.
F — Description of ancillary equipment controlled by the fire alarm system.
G — In systems that provide logical control of a smoke control system, documentation is on site and includes a description of the operation of the smoke control system.
H — Building diagrams are on site that clearly indicate the type and location of all smoke control equipment (fans, dampers, etc.).
I — Description of the fire alarm system:
i) Sequence of Operation (See Annex D).
ii) Operating Instructions (See Annex D).
iii) Description of each type of field device.
IV) Details of input to programmed output functions for programmed systems.
V) Connection to a fire signal receiving centre, if required by applicable codes and regulations.
VI) Previous verification report(s) and all documentation related to any modification showing approval of such modifications by the AHJ, if applicable.
VII) The as-built drawings of the building fire alarm system (See Annex D).
VIII) Copy of the site specific software (if applicable).
J — Indicate the location(s) and media type(s) of documentation:

sections.title: Control Unit or Transponder Test Record
sections.description: Repeatable section — complete for each control unit or transponder on the system.

fields.label: Control Unit or Transponder Test Record

notes.label: ✗ = Did not operate correctly (Refer to Deficiencies, 20.2)
— = Not applicable — Function or feature not provided on this fire alarm system

subsections.title: Control Unit or Transponder Inspection Record

fields.label: Field Location
Control Unit / Transponder ID
A — Input circuit designations correctly identified in relation to connected field devices.
B — Output circuit designations correctly identified in relation to field devices.
C — Correct designations for common control functions and indicators.
D — Plug-In components and modules securely in place.
E — Plug-In cables securely in place.
F — Firmware Date
F — Firmware Revision
F — Firmware Version
F — Software Date
F — Software Revision
F — Software Version
G — Clean and free of dust and dirt.
H — Fuses in accordance with manufacturer's specification.
I — Control unit or transponder lock functional.
J — Termination points from wiring to field devices secure.

subsections.title: Control Unit or Transponder Functional Test

fields.label: A — Power 'on' visual indicator operates.
B — Time and date indication corresponds with local time and date.
C — Common visual trouble signal operates.
D — Common audible trouble signal operates.
E — Trouble signal silence switch operates.
F — Main power supply failure trouble signal operates.
G — Ground fault on positive and negative conductors initiates trouble signal.
H — Alert signal operates.
I — Alarm signal operates.
J — Automatic transfer from alert to alarm signal operates.
J — Auto-transfer time (seconds)
K — Manual transfer from alert to alarm signal operates.
L — Auto transfer from alert to alarm signal cancel (acknowledge) operates on two-stage system.
M — Alarm signal silence inhibit function operates.
N — Alarm signal manual silence operates.
O — Alarm signal silence visual indication operates.
P — Silenced alarm signal automatically re-initiates only on subsequent alarm from another NBC-required FA zone.
Q — Duration of alarm signal prior to automatic silence (seconds)
R — Audible/visual alert and alarm signals programmed and operate per design / documentation.
S — Input circuit alarm and supervisory operation (audible and visual) operates.
T — Input circuit supervision fault causes trouble indication.
U — Output circuit alarm indicators operate.
V — Output circuit supervision fault causes trouble indication.
W — Visual indicator test (lamp test) operates.
X — Coded signal sequence operates ≥ required number of times with correct alarm signal thereafter.
Y — Coded signal sequences are not interrupted by subsequent alarms.
Z — Ancillary device control circuit is rated for intended purpose.
AA — Ancillary device bypass results in trouble signal.
BB — Input-to-output operation including ancillary device operates per design / Annex D documentation.
CC — System Reset operates.
DD — Main power to emergency power supply transfer operates.
EE — Smoke detector alarm verification (status change confirmation) verified.

applicable_toggle.label: Control Unit or Transponder Functional Test

fields.label: Location
Identification
A — Power 'on' visual indicator operates.
B — Common visual trouble signal operates.
C — Common audible trouble signal operates.
D — Trouble signal silence switch operates.
E — All-call voice paging (including visual indicator) operates.
F — Selective voice paging output circuits (including visual indication) operate.
G — Selective voice paging trouble operation (including visual indication) operates.
H — Microphone (including press-to-talk switch) operates.
I — Voice paging does not interfere with initial inhibit time of alert and alarm signals.
J — All-call voice paging operates on emergency power supply.
K — Back-up amplifier automatic transfer feature operates (where backup amps installed).
L — Emergency telephone call-in circuits (audible and visual indication) operate.
M — Emergency telephone two-way voice communication circuits operate.
N — Emergency telephone trouble operation (visual indication) operates.
O — Emergency telephone verbal communication operates.
P — Emergency telephone operable / in-use tone at handset operates.
Q — Short or open on paging/alert/alarm/emergency telephone voice bus in standby results in bus-specific trouble.
Power Supply Field Location
Power Supply Identification
Circuit Disconnect Means Location
Panel / Breaker Identification
A — Fused in accordance with manufacturer's marked rating.
B — Primary supply equipped with identified disconnect means.
C — Adequate to meet system requirements.
D — Short on isolated side of each power isolation module results in trouble condition.
E — Operation of device on source side of each shorted power isolation module is confirmed.
F — Ancillary device power taken from a source separate from the FACU or transponder (if separate supply).
G — Ancillary device power taken from the FACU/transponder designed to provide such power (if integrated).
H — Ancillary devices powered from FACU/transponder are recorded.

subsections.title: Emergency Power Supply Test and Inspection

fields.label: Emergency Power Supply Field Location
Emergency Power Supply Identification
Emergency Power Supply Type
Battery Type
Battery Capacity (AH as installed)
NBC Required Full Load Alarm Operation Time
A — Correct battery type as recommended by manufacturer.
B — Correct battery rating per battery calculations based on full system load.
C — Battery voltage (main power ON) (VDC)
C — Battery charging current (main power ON) (mA)
D — Battery voltage (main power OFF, FAS supervisory) (VDC)
D — Battery current (main power OFF, FAS supervisory) (mA)
E — Battery voltage (main power OFF, FAS full load ALARM) (VDC)
E — Battery current (main power OFF, FAS full load ALARM) (A)
F — Battery free of physical damage.
G — Battery terminals cleaned and lubricated.
H — Battery terminals clamped tightly.
I — Correct electrolyte level.
J — Specific gravity of electrolyte within manufacturer's specifications.
K — Inspected for electrolyte leakage.
L — Adequately ventilated.
M — Manufacturer's date code or in-service date
N — Battery disconnection causes trouble signal.
O — Battery test method performed on fully charged battery
options: Required supervisory load for 24h then full load operation

fields.label: O — Battery manufacturer's method (specify)
P — Calculated battery capacity (Annex C2) (AH)
Q — Battery terminal voltage after tests (VDC)
R — Battery voltage ≥ 85% of rated capacity after tests.
S — Battery charging current after tests (A)
Generator A — Generator provides power to AC circuit serving FAS.
Generator B — Trouble conditions at generator result in audible common trouble signal and visual indication.
Generator C — Generator 'Run' condition results in audible common trouble signal and visual indication.

applicable_toggle.label: Emergency Power Supply Test and Inspection

subsections.title: Annunciator, Remote Trouble Signal Unit, Display & Control Centre Test and Inspection

applicable_toggle.label: Annunciator, Remote Trouble Signal Unit, Display & Control Centre Test and Inspection

fields.label: Annunciator Location
Annunciator Identification
A — Power 'on' indicator operates.
B — Individual alarm and supervisory input zones clearly indicated and separately designated.
C — Individual alarm and supervisory input zone designation labels properly identified.
D — Device labels correspond with actual field location (active and supporting field devices).
E — Common trouble signal operates.
F — Visual indicator test (lamp test) operates.
G — Input wiring from control unit or transponder is supervised.
H — Alarm signal silence visual indicator operates.
I — Switches for ancillary functions operate per design / Annex D documentation.
J — Ancillary functions visual indicators operate.
K — Manual activation of alarm signal and indication operates.
L — Displays are visible in the installed location.
M — Operates on emergency power.
Annunciator / Sequential Display Location
Annunciator / Sequential Display Identification
A — Power 'on' indicator operates.
B — Individual alarm and supervisory zone designation labels properly identified.
C — Individual alarm and supervisory indications properly identified (where individually annunciated).
D — Device location and programmed label/descriptor confirmed (active and supporting devices).
E — Common trouble signal operates.
F — Visual indicator test (lamp test) operates.
G — Input wiring from control unit or transponder is supervised.
H — Alarm signal silence visual indicator operates.
I — Switches for ancillary functions operate per design / documentation (Section 21).
J — Ancillary functions visual indicators operate.
K — Manual activation of alarm signal and indication operates.
L — Displays are visible in the installed location.
M — Multi-line sequential display operates per Section 10.2 (where utilized).

subsections.title: Remote Trouble Signal Unit Test and Inspection

applicable_toggle.label: Remote Trouble Signal Unit Test and Inspection

fields.label: Remote Trouble Signal Unit Location
Remote Trouble Signal Unit Identification
A — Input wiring from control unit or transponder is supervised.
B — Visual trouble signal operates.
C — Audible trouble signal operates.
D — Audible trouble signal silence operates.
Printer Location
Printer Identification
A — Operates per design / Annex D documentation.
B — Zone of each alarm initiating device is correctly printed.

sections.title: Ancillary Device Circuit Test
sections.description: Tabular record of each ancillary circuit tested. Repeatable rows.

applicable_toggle.label: Ancillary device circuits exist on this system

sections.title: Interconnection to the Fire Signal Receiving Centre

applicable_toggle.label: System has interconnection to a Fire Signal Receiving Centre

fields.label: Communicator Location
Circuit Disconnect Means Location
Panel / Breaker Identification
A — FSRC transmitter is integral to the FACU.
A — Supervised interconnection between FACU and a separately installed FSRC transmitter is provided.
B — Alarm transmission to FSRC is received.
C — Supervisory transmission to FSRC is received.
D — Trouble transmission to FSRC is received.
E — Disabling/disconnecting FSRC transmitter results in specific trouble signal at control unit.
F — Disabling/disconnecting FSRC transmitter transmits trouble signal to FSRC.
G — FSRC Company Name
G — FSRC Telephone
G — FSRC Address
H — Operation of FSRC disconnect means transmits trouble signal to FSRC.

applicable_toggle.label: Data Communication Link (DCL) circuits exist on this system

fields.label: Control Unit or Transponder Location
Control Unit or Transponder Identification
Data Communication Link Circuit ID
A — Each system abnormal condition (Table 3.1) tested for each DCL at the control unit / transponder.
B — Alarm and trouble tests under single ground fault conducted on each conductor independently.
C — Each conductor in a DCLA tested for alarm signal capability on each side of a single open fault.
D — Wire-to-wire short fault tested on each DCL without fault isolation; trouble and alarm from adjacent DCL confirmed.
E — Short on isolated side of fault isolators confirmed; device operation from source side confirmed at control unit.
F — Where fault isolation is between control units/transponders, shorted section confirmed and operation outside confirmed.

subsections.title: Field Device Testing — Legend
subsections.description: Device type codes reference table. Used to populate Device Type in the Individual Device Record.
subsections.mobile_note: This is a read-only reference displayed as an in-app glossary or searchable picker. Not a fillable section.

subsections.title: Individual Device Record
subsections.description: One row per field device. Repeatable. Legend: ✓ = Acceptable, ✗ = Unacceptable (log in §20.2), — = Not Applicable.

item_fields.label: Device Type
item_fields.hint: Select from legend or type code

item_fields.label: Requires Service, Repairs, Cleaning, or Missing
Correctly Installed (per ULC-S524 at time of installation)
Measurements
item_fields.hint: Sensitivity reading, time delay, pressure, flow delay, date changed, etc.

subsections.title: Circuit Fault Tolerance Test Sheet
subsections.description: Records fault tolerance test results per DCL circuit. Repeatable rows.

applicable_toggle.label: Circuit fault tolerance testing applies to this system (addressable / Class A systems)

item_fields.label: Device Location Where Fault Was Introduced / Affected NBC FA Zone Description
Isolation Results — Identify NBC FA Zone / Area Where Devices Failed
Non-Faulted Circuit — Individual Device Location Tested

field_type_definitions.date: Date picker (YYYY-MM-DD storage; display as MM/DD/YY per standard)
field_type_definitions.tri_state: Three-option selector: Pass (✓) / Fail (✗) / N/A (—). The standard's core test result type.
field_type_definitions.pass_fail: Two-option selector: Pass (P) / Fail (F). Used in Circuit Fault Tolerance (23.3).
field_type_definitions.radio: Single-select from a fixed list of options
field_type_definitions.repeatable_list: Dynamic list of identical item-cards (add / remove / reorder)
field_type_definitions.repeatable_section: Full sub-form that can be instantiated multiple times (e.g., one per control unit)
